title: The Hero Truong Dinh
summary: Truong Dinh chose to disobey the King's order rather than abandon the people in the resistance against the French in the Southern region.

slides.text: When the royal court signed a peace treaty with the French and ordered Truong Dinh to disband his troops, the people instead honored him as the 'Binh Tay Dai Nguyen Soai' (Great Commander Pacificator of the West).
slides.imagePrompt: People presenting a commander flag to Truong Dinh, huge crowd in Nam Bo landscape, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: He bravely declared: 'To save the country, one must rather disobey the King', and continued to lead the insurgent troops against the French.
slides.imagePrompt: Truong Dinh holding his sword, looking towards his soldiers, determined and resolute, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Under his command, the insurgent army used guerrilla warfare, causing the French troops many sleepless nights.
slides.imagePrompt: Insurrection army hiding in dense bamboo groves, preparing an ambush for French soldiers, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Truong Dinh is a symbol of the spirit of patriotism and selfless devotion to the people of the heroic Southern region.
slides.imagePrompt: Truong Dinh's memorial statue with blooming flowers, peaceful countryside background, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: What did the people honor Truong Dinh as?
options: Great Commander Pacificator of the West
slides.answer: Great Commander Pacificator of the West

slides.question: In which region did Truong Dinh lead the resistance against the French?
